nodes.label_negative: AI as writer of full chapters

nodes.label: Open AI use in writing community

nodes.label: AI as threat to writer livelihood
nodes.label_negative: AI as efficiency tool that doesn't replace human writers
nodes.grounding_span: a lot of people think that AI is going to put writers out of work

nodes.label: AI handling grunt work to free up creative writing
nodes.label_negative: AI encroaching on creative writing itself
nodes.grounding_span: If I can use AI to do all the grunt work... then it frees up more time for me to do the thing I enjoy most

nodes.label: Creative autonomy and human nuance
nodes.grounding_span: the small creative details that I like to add in myself

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: allows me to write more efficiently

nodes.label: Social acceptance and professional reputation
nodes.grounding_span: I fear the backlash that could come with anyone knowing I'm using AI

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for outlines and brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: I've had a lot of success with using AI for plot lines and brainstorming

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for full chapter writing
Cautious secretive use due to social pressure
nodes.grounding_span: I approach these tools with caution... essentially forcing me to use it secretly

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future personal assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I can see myself using it to create book covers... as a personal assistant of sorts

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: I often pick out specific things and ask it to tweak specific spots
